classical_node_name: Sexually dimorphic nucleus of the preoptic area (SDN-POA) calbindin neuron

0.name: 0666 Sncg Gaba_1
0.rationale: GABAergic Sncg cluster (cortex/hippocampus lineage) with male_female_ratio 1.27 — mildly male-biased but insufficient for SDN-POA. No MPN/POA anatomical provenance. Calb1 match may reflect broad Calb1 expression in Sncg interneurons rather than specific SDN-POA identity. Parent SUPT_0185 is a cortical/hippocampal Sncg supertype. No Th in name (negative marker not triggered). Not a viable candidate.

0.name: 0668 Sncg Gaba_1
0.rationale: Same parent supertype (SUPT_0185) and region as CLUS_0666. Male_female_ratio 1.17 — no sex bias. Cortical/hippocampal Sncg interneuron; no preoptic provenance. Calb1 hit likely incidental. Not a viable candidate.

0.name: 0744 Pvalb Gaba_4

1.name: 0423 BST-MPN Six3 Nrgn Gaba_4
1.rationale: Only rank-1 candidate with MPN (medial preoptic nucleus) directly in the label. BST-MPN lineage is the correct broad anatomical zone for SDN-POA (MBA:464). GABAergic identity is consistent with the literature description of SDN-POA as a GABAergic population. Six3 is a transcription factor expressed in preoptic/hypothalamic GABAergic neurons; Nrgn (neurogranin) is a calcium signaling protein. No Th in label (negative marker not triggered). No sex ratio data at supertype level. The Calb1 marker match that drove DB retrieval is plausible for MPN GABAergic neurons. KEY CAVEAT: supertype-level resolution means multiple distinct MPN cell types are collapsed here; Calb1-specific SDN-POA cells may be only a subset of this supertype. Requires cluster-level confirmation. Best anatomical match in the candidate set. Parent: CS20230722_SUBC_090.

1.name: 0288 STR-PAL Chst9 Gaba_4
1.rationale: Striatum-pallidum (STR-PAL) GABAergic supertype. Pallidum is adjacent to preoptic area but STR-PAL supertypes in WMBv1 typically refer to striatal/pallidal subpallial lineage, not MPN proper. Calb1 is expressed in medium spiny neurons and pallidal neurons so the marker match may be incidental. Anatomically plausible only if 'PAL' extends into substantia innominata/preoptic region, but this is uncertain without cluster-level inspection. Treat as WEAK pending cluster resolution. Parent: CS20230722_SUBC_064.

1.name: 0496 LGv-ZI Otx2 Gaba_1
1.rationale: Lateral geniculate ventral / zona incerta (LGv-ZI) GABAergic supertype. Zona incerta is adjacent to the hypothalamus but distant from MPN/POA. Otx2 expression in zona incerta GABAergic neurons does not align with known SDN-POA markers. ADJACENT region at best; not preoptic. Parent: CS20230722_SUBC_109.

1.name: 0227 Sst Gaba_14
1.rationale: Sst (somatostatin) GABAergic cortical/hippocampal supertype. Calb1 and Sst can co-occur in cortical interneurons but this supertype has no preoptic anatomical provenance. Parent: CS20230722_SUBC_053.

1.name: 0225 Sst Gaba_12
1.rationale: Same parent (SUBC_053) and reasoning as SUPT_0227. Sst Gaba cortical supertype; no preoptic provenance.

1.name: 0143 OB Meis2 Thsd7b Gaba_1
1.rationale: Olfactory bulb (OB) — distant from MBA:464. Olfactory bulb interneurons express Calb1 broadly, making the marker match incidental. Not a viable candidate.

1.name: 0525 LHA Barhl2 Glut_2
1.rationale: Lateral hypothalamic area (LHA) — glutamatergic, adjacent to MPN but distinct region. SDN-POA is GABAergic and localized to MPN, not LHA. Not a viable candidate.

1.name: 0704 PAG-MRN Pou3f1 Glut_1
1.rationale: Periaqueductal gray / midbrain reticular nucleus — distant mesencephalon. Glutamatergic. Not a viable candidate.

1.name: 0925 PCG-PRNr Vsx2 Nkx6-1 Glut_1
1.rationale: Pontine central gray / pontine reticular nucleus — distant brainstem. Glutamatergic. Not a viable candidate.

1.name: 0906 LDT-PCG Vsx2 Lhx4 Glut_1
1.rationale: Laterodorsal tegmentum / pontine central gray — distant brainstem. Glutamatergic. Not a viable candidate.

1.name: 1048 CS-PRNr-DR En1 Sox2 Gaba_2
1.rationale: Central superior raphe / pontine reticular nucleus / dorsal raphe — distant brainstem. GABAergic but wrong region entirely. Not a viable candidate.

non_candidates_summary: All 30 rank-0 candidates are anatomically incompatible with SDN-POA (MBA:464 / MPN). The DB score of 1 reflects only a Calb1 marker hit — Calb1 is broadly expressed across the brain (cortical interneurons, cerebellar Purkinje, olfactory bulb, hippocampus) so this marker alone does not constrain location. None of the rank-0 clusters carry MPN, POA, or preoptic provenance in their labels. The critical limitation is that the DB query scored only on marker and location but Calb1 does not uniquely identify preoptic neurons. At rank-1, CS20230722_SUPT_0423 (BST-MPN Six3 Nrgn Gaba_4) is the sole anatomically viable candidate. The negative marker Th is absent from all candidate labels, so no negative-marker penalty applies. However, the weak overall candidate quality reflects that SDN-POA Calb1 neurons may be an unrepresented or very small cluster in WMBv1 (consistent with the SDN-POA being a nucleus estimated at a few hundred cells in mouse). Consider running annotation-transfer at cluster level within SUPT_0423 lineage to locate Calb1-enriched MPN subclusters.
